mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or cumulative drawdown during trigger periods is less than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or 60-day forward returns fail to exceed 1% during concurrent expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in industrial production reflect robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum during tightening periods, or rolling IC drops below 0.02 over 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations above the threshold, or mean return difference between above/below threshold is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total payrolls and rising unemployment indicates declining job quality and hidden labor market weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with > 50% accuracy over 60-day horizons, or interaction effect vanishes in multivariate walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal immediate construction sector activity and broader economic confidence, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with HOUST momentum, or predictive accuracy falls below 50% across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its long-term trend baseline indicates systemic supply chain constraints and manufacturing contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100.0, or mean return during sub-trend periods is not significantly negative.
